title: Cognitive Restructuring
description: Identify and challenge negative automatic thoughts
steps: Examine the evidence for and against
example_dialogue:
Therapist: What went through your mind when you felt upset?
Client: I thought 'I'm a complete failure'
Therapist: Let's examine the evidence. What are some things you've succeeded at recently?

title: Behavioral Activation
description: Increase engagement in positively reinforcing activities
steps: Identify activities that bring pleasure or achievement
Review and adjust based on mood impact
example_dialogue:
Therapist: Even small activities can help break the depression cycle. What's one thing you used to enjoy?
Client: I used to like walking in the park.
Therapist: Could we schedule a 10-minute walk for tomorrow morning?

title: Mindfulness and Grounding
description: Present-moment awareness techniques for managing distress
example_dialogue: Therapist: When you feel overwhelmed, try the 5-4-3-2-1 grounding technique. It helps bring you back to the present moment.

title: Sleep Hygiene Protocol
description: Establish healthy sleep patterns to support mental health
steps: Limit screen time 1 hour before bed
Use bed only for sleep and rest
example_dialogue: Therapist: Sleep disruption is common with depression. Let's work on a consistent routine. What time could you commit to going to bed each night?

title: Crisis Safety Planning
description: Create a structured plan for managing crisis moments
steps: People and social settings for distraction
example_dialogue: Therapist: Let's create a safety plan together. What are some early signs that you're heading toward a crisis?

title: Graded Exposure
description: Gradually face feared situations to reduce anxiety
steps: Create a fear hierarchy (least to most scary)
Stay in the situation until anxiety drops
example_dialogue: Therapist: We'll take this one step at a time. What's the smallest, least scary situation we could start with?

title: Values Clarification
description: Identify personal values to guide meaningful action
steps: Explore what truly matters to you
Identify gaps between values and actions
example_dialogue: Therapist: When depression lifts, what kind of person do you want to be? What matters most to you?

title: Thought Record
description: Structured journal for analyzing and reframing thoughts
example_dialogue: Therapist: Let's write down exactly what happened and what you thought. Sometimes seeing it on paper helps us challenge it.
